text:
Market today: The market mood is cautious. Geopolitical tensions are rising with Iran's expanded attacks, but the real driver for your book is the semiconductor sector's slump. This is directly impacting your concentrated tech/chip names.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down -44.02% and highly leveraged. SPAL, a new leveraged ETF, is also deeply in the red at -41.51%. Remember, margin amplifies these drops. QQQ and SMH are also showing negative P&L and are on TRIM status.

Watch: Keep a close eye on general semiconductor news, especially around AI-adjacent sectors. Also, monitor your leveraged positions, SOXL and SPAL, for any further downside.

Bottom line: Manage your risk on leveraged, underperforming positions in a slumping chip market.
plays: SOXL is deeply underwater and leveraged; consider trimming further or setting a tighter, active stop below current price to protect against more decay.
SPAL is a new, leveraged position down significantly; review its stop at 21.38 and consider if the risk/reward still aligns with your tolerance.
QQQ and SMH are on TRIM status and slightly negative; evaluate if their current stops at 670 and 540 are appropriate for managing risk.
